verdicts.issue: ПДР 19.1 requires a moving mechanical TZ in poor visibility to have low (high) beam ON, with fog lights only ADDITIONALLY permitted. Fog lights alone do not satisfy the requirement. Option 3 ('Фари ближнього (дального) світла. Дозволяється увімкнути протитуманні фари як окремо, так і разом...') is the option that fully states the ПДР rule and is the likely correct answer. The marked answer 'Протитуманні фари' (fog lights alone) appears wrong. Confidence moderate because some ГСЦ base variants pair this stem with a different option set; the explanation itself reads as strained, hinting the key may be off.

Marked key 'На жорсткому зчепленні або способом часткового навантаження' is correct (ПДР п.23.4 allows rigid coupling OR partial-loading for a vehicle with non-functioning steering). But short+detailed claim ONLY partial-loading is allowed and explicitly say rigid coupling does NOT meet the requirement — this contradicts and undermines the correct marked answer. Explanation needs regen to state both rigid coupling and partial loading are permitted.
Conclusion correct (descending vehicle yields); detailed rationale phrasing is slightly imprecise but not misleading.

Marked answer 'Дозволено' is correct and the explanation about the 'обслуговування' exception is consistent with ПДР sign exemptions (3.21/3.22 etc.). However the question text is oddly truncated/contextless (likely tied to a specific sign image not shown here), so the explanation generalizes a context it can't fully see — safe to display but justification is speculative about which sign applies.

Marked answer 'Ні' is correct: Ukrainian law sets a single 0.2 permille blood-alcohol threshold for all drivers regardless of experience, so the 0.3 permille claim in the question is false. Explanation accurately cites 0.2 permille.
6000 km clutch-check interval is plausible per the exam base but I cannot independently confirm the exact figure; explanation is safe.
Verified against ПДР п.12.6 — mopeds are limited to 60 km/h outside settlements. Marked answer 60 km/h is correct.

Marked answer 250 m. Standard ПДР dazzle-switch distance for cars is 'not less than 250 m'; this motorcycle item uses the same figure, consistent with the official base. Lower confidence as the exact figure is hard to confirm independently, but 250 m matches the canonical ПДР value.

Answer 'Більше дев'яти' is correct: category B is limited to vehicles with up to 8 seats besides the driver, so the MAXIMUM number of passengers a B driver may carry exceeds 9 only when phrased about seats. The short text is self-contradictory/confusing: it first says 'кількості сидячих місць більше дев'яти' then parenthetically '(включно з водієм — не більш як вісім місць крім водія)'. The marked answer matches the official base, but the justification conflates passenger count with seat count and is imprecise; safe to show but weak.

Answer 'Дозволено' is right, but explanation is generic boilerplate ('навіть якщо для іншого транспорту він був би обмежений') with no reference to the actual depicted sign/markings; safe to show but weak justification for an image-based question.
Marked answer 'Дозволено' is consistent with marshrut-ТЗ allowances, but explanation is pure boilerplate about route exemptions without describing the depicted situation; safe but uninformative.
Answer 'Дозволено' is right; explanation is generic ('якщо знаки та розмітка не забороняють') and doesn't ground itself in the depicted scene. Safe to show, weak justification.
Weekly driving time incl. overtime max 48 h is the standard road-transport norm; answer and explanation consistent.
Max length of coupled (towed) route TVs is 30 m; detailed is null but short correctly states the answer.
Route TV over 25 m must move by special (oversized) rules; consistent with ПДР large-size limits.
Route 500 km+ beyond settlement requires two drivers; standard professional-driver regime norm.
Double-crew daily rest min 8 consecutive hours; consistent with the regime norms.
Daily (inter-shift) rest within any 24-h period min 10 consecutive hours for single driver; matches norm and contrasts correctly with 8 h double-crew case.
Transport of organized groups of children in the dark is prohibited per ПДР 22; the third option contains an embedded section-header artifact but the marked answer and explanation are correct.
Pressure-limiting valve reduces pressure in the front-axle brake chambers (limits front braking force to prevent steered-wheel lockup); answer and explanation correct.
